title: Change the field type of the subitem column?
excerpt:
I’d like to change the field type of the subitem column to a dropdown.  Is that possible? 
Not having seen a way to do that, I’ve created another column as a dropdown, and then I tried to create an automation to change t…

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to change the field type of the subitem column to a dropdown. Is that possible?

Not having seen a way to do that, I’ve created another column as a dropdown, and then I tried to create an automation to change the value of the subitem column to be assigned the value of my new column. But I also could not find a way to do that.

As a 3rd option, I’d like to autofill the subitem column with an auto-incrementing integer. Is that possible?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you click on the three dots at the top of that subitem column, you should have an option to ‘Change Column Type’. This example shows that with a dropdown column being one that you can choose from.

image1238×403 44.7 KB

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: @Clover_Gal , looking at your reply once more, I see that you are showing the popups for the text column. I’m asking about the “Subitem” column specifically (the left-most column in your screenshot). This is the column I want to change.

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are no ellipses in the SubItem column when I hover over it. Below is what I see. Maybe this is because we are on the free version of monday.com?

image.png600×300 17.2 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it, so you can’t change the type of column for the ‘first’ column, no matter which plan you’re on.

In regards to your automations, you could likely find a way to do something similar to what you’re looking for depending which plan/product you have. Do you have access to workflows/automations? Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the follow-up. We’re using Work Management. I do have access to automations. We are a non-profit on a free plan.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you always have/need the same number of subitems for each item or does it vary? ie you could create an automation to create the subitems numbered 1-10 (or whatever #). Would be a bit of a set up the first time, but would keep you from having to manually do it later.

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your continued attention to this. The number of subitems will vary.
